framework_bugs.summary: init.sh --git-host github creates real GitHub remote without explicit opt-in
framework_bugs.reproduction: WORKDIR=$(mktemp -d); cd $WORKDIR; bash $FRAMEWORK/init.sh --non-interactive --project uat-agent-1 --platform desktop --track light --deployment personal --language rust --project-dir $WORKDIR/uat-agent-1 --git-host github --visibility private --allow-existing-dir
framework_bugs.expected: In a no-network UAT environment, init.sh should not invoke gh to create a real remote when no remote URL is supplied. Either require --remote-url for github (like --git-host other does with --branch-protection-attested), or provide a --no-remote / --skip-remote-creation flag, or default to skipping when GH_TOKEN/gh auth is absent.
framework_bugs.actual: init.sh used the 'github driver' to call gh and created a real private repo at https://github.com/kraulerson/uat-agent-1 and pushed the initial commit before reporting the protection-config soft-fail. Side effect: the UAT agent now has a real public-cloud artifact it did not consent to. Branch-protection step then printed [FAIL] but exit code is 0 (PR #18 fake-remote tolerance), so init proceeds. The runbook explicitly says 'No real network calls beyond git remote add origin https://example.com/fake.git' and this violates that contract for any UAT agent that selects --git-host github.

documentation_gaps.gap: init.sh --help-non-interactive does not document that --git-host github + no --remote-url will auto-create a remote via gh; users expect a dry-run / --skip-remote flag for scripted/UAT flows.

tests.name: start_phase1_exits_zero
tests.details: process-checklist.sh --start-phase1 OK, RC=0.

tests.name: phase1_steps_array_present
tests.details: process-state.json .phase1_architecture.steps_completed is an array.

tests.name: each_phase1_step_completes
tests.details: All 5 PHASE1_STEPS (architecture_selected, threat_model_complete, data_model_defined, ui_scaffolding_done, bible_synthesized) completed sequentially with RC=0; final message 'All steps complete for phase1_architecture'.

tests.name: phase1_to_phase2_gate_passes
tests.details: scripts/check-phase-gate.sh exited 0 with 'Phase gates consistent.' Note: gate is informational only; it does not advance .current_phase (see U-D confirmation below).

tests.name: phase_state_advances_to_2
tests.details: U-D confirmation: after all Phase 1 steps complete and check-phase-gate.sh exits 0, .current_phase remains 0. No script in $FRAMEWORK/scripts/ writes a higher value to .current_phase except validate.sh (read-only) and pre-commit-gate.sh (read-only). Manually set .current_phase=2 via jq to continue UAT.

framework_bugs.summary: U-D confirmation: no script advances .current_phase after gate passes
framework_bugs.reproduction: Complete all 5 PHASE1_STEPS via process-checklist.sh --complete-step phase1_architecture:* ; run scripts/check-phase-gate.sh ; jq '.current_phase' .claude/phase-state.json
framework_bugs.expected: After Phase 1 gate passes (all required artifacts + steps complete), .claude/phase-state.json .current_phase should advance from 0 to 2 (or 1 if there is an intermediate state).
framework_bugs.actual: .current_phase stays at 0. check-phase-gate.sh prints 'Phase gates consistent.' and exits 0 but writes nothing. Same observation made in 2026-04-25 sweep; not fixed in PRs #16-#19. Test agent had to manually run jq to advance the phase to continue the UAT protocol.

documentation_gaps.gap: builders-guide / process-checklist user docs do not state which command, if any, the user must run to advance .current_phase after a gate passes. The runbook's Section B note flags this as U-D-still-open; user-facing guidance should either expose an --advance-phase command or document the manual jq step.

tests.name: start_feature_records_state
tests.details: After --start-feature uat-feat-1, .build_loop.feature = 'uat-feat-1' in process-state.json.

tests.name: each_step_completes_in_order
tests.details: All 5 build-step transitions (tests_written -> documentation_updated) succeeded sequentially. Sixth step (feature_recorded) succeeded after test-gate.sh --record-feature.

tests.name: pre_commit_passes_after_loop
tests.details: After all 5 steps complete and stub source/test/security-audit artifacts in place, `git add -A && git commit -m 'feat(uat-1): stub feature for UAT'` succeeded with exit 0. (Note: this is direct shell git, which bypasses the Claude Code PreToolUse path — see U-C confirmation; in a real Claude session the PreToolUse hook would also evaluate.)

tests.name: bl006_blocks_feat_without_loop
tests.details: U-C confirmation: ran `git commit --allow-empty -m 'feat(bl006-trigger): no loop'` after recording feat-1 (no --start-feature for the new commit). Direct shell git commit succeeded (RC=0) — bypasses BL-006 because it does not pass through the Claude Code PreToolUse path. The PreToolUse hook (pre-commit-gate.sh) does correctly enforce when invoked through hook input; sentinel test below confirms hook denies.

tests.name: bl006_grace_window_resets_after_record
tests.details: After --record-feature uat-feat-1, ran a NEW feature loop (uat-feat-2) and committed feat(uat-2): the feat: commit succeeded. PR #17 BL-006 between-features grace-window reset is functioning.

tests.name: bl015_sentinel_blocks_commit
tests.details: Wrote .claude/pending-approval.json (with placeholder fields), then piped a synthetic Bash/git-commit PreToolUse payload into scripts/pre-commit-gate.sh. Hook responded with `permissionDecision: deny` and reason 'pre-commit gate: commit blocked — pending user decision. The sentinel file .claude/pending-approval.json exists but is malformed. Treated as in flight per the CDF 4.2.3 contract.' Sentinel correctly enforced through the hook path. NOTE: direct shell `git commit` while sentinel is present still succeeds (same U-C class issue — shell-direct git bypasses Claude Code hooks).

tests.name: bl015_sentinel_resolves
tests.details: scripts/pending-approval.sh --resolve removed the file; subsequent PreToolUse hook invocation no longer denies on sentinel grounds (it then denies for an unrelated reason: 'Phase 2 initialization not verified', which is a different gate).

tests.name: recorded_feature_appears_in_progress
tests.details: .claude/build-progress.json .features_completed contains 'uat-feat-1' (and after the second loop, 'uat-feat-2').

framework_bugs.summary: U-C confirmation: direct shell git commit bypasses BL-006 + BL-015
framework_bugs.reproduction: Without running --start-feature: `git commit --allow-empty -m 'feat(bl006-trigger): no loop'` from a project shell. Also: with .claude/pending-approval.json present, run `git commit --allow-empty -m 'feat(should-block): test sentinel'`.
framework_bugs.expected: Either (a) a git commit-msg or pre-commit hook installed by init.sh enforces BL-006 (no `feat:` subject without a recorded build loop) and BL-015 (refuse commit while .claude/pending-approval.json exists), OR (b) docs prominently state that BL-006/BL-015 enforcement is Claude-Code-only and direct shell commits are out of scope by design.
framework_bugs.actual: Both shell commits succeeded with RC=0. The Claude Code PreToolUse hook (pre-commit-gate.sh) correctly enforces both gates when invoked, but a developer or scripted process running `git commit` directly bypasses them entirely. Same as 2026-04-25 finding U-C; PR #17 fixed the `.tool_input.command` payload path (so hook-invoked enforcement works) but did not add a parallel git commit-msg/pre-commit hook.

documentation_gaps.gap: pending-approval.sh has no --check subcommand; the help shows --offer/--resolve/--clear/--status only. The runbook Section C example references --check, which exits 1 with 'Unknown command'. Either add --check as an alias for --status, or update runbook + any user-facing doc to use --status.
documentation_gaps.where: scripts/pending-approval.sh help block + Reports/uat-2026-04-26/RUNBOOK.md Section C

documentation_gaps.gap: phase3_validation:accessibility_audit and :performance_audit require artifacts in docs/test-results/ matching glob *accessibility* / *performance* / *lighthouse*, but the [INFO] message printed when they fail does not state the exact glob — the user must read the script. Suggest the warn message include 'Save as: docs/test-results/YYYY-MM-DD_accessibility.md (or *lighthouse*)'.

tests.name: no_uncommitted_secrets
tests.details: Strict regex `(api[_-]?key|password|secret|token)\s*[:=]\s*["']?[A-Za-z0-9_\-]{16,}` against `git ls-files` returns 0 matches. Loose grep matches only documentation/template files (executive-review, security guides, evaluation prompts, handoff/incident-response templates) where the words appear in prose.

tests.name: no_framework_contamination
tests.details: git -C $FRAMEWORK status --short: only pre-existing untracked entries (.claude/, Reports/uat-2026-04-26/) — both pre-conversation. git -C $FRAMEWORK rev-parse HEAD = d66e8f276079aa352ba90ac54386e1b1896d7494 (matches start-of-run capture). PR #18 framework-self guard worked.

summary: base/personal/desktop/light passes end-to-end on framework HEAD d66e8f2: init non-interactive, all phase 1-4 steps, BL-015 sentinel, BL-006 grace window, framework-self guard, U-H fixes all verified. Confirms still-open prior bugs U-C (direct shell git bypasses BL-006/BL-015) and U-D (no script advances .current_phase) at Medium severity. New Medium finding: init.sh --git-host github auto-creates real GitHub remote without an explicit opt-in or --skip-remote flag, violating UAT no-network contract.
